Live streaming platforms

Beautifully powerful streaming. Astonishingly easy. DualStream brings Desktop and Mobile audiences your content at the same time, from a single patent-pending engine. DualStream Relay carries your broadcast to every platform from our servers — and if your connection blinks, disconnection protection keeps your stream alive. Native Alerts, Per-Source audio control, VTuber support, instant replay clips, cinema grade effects, pop-out multi-platform chat and so much more is all built in. [...] DualStream is one studio that streams to your Desktop and Mobile audiences at the same time - same sources, arranged for each scene, live everywhere in one click. The DualStream Relay - one upload from your PC, live on every platform from our servers. And if your connection drops mid-broadcast, Relay keeps every platform live on your standby screen and picks back up the moment you reconnect. Native Alerts, Per-Source audio control, VTuber support, instant replay clips (horizontal and vertical), cinema grade effects, pop-out multi-platform chat and so much more is all built in. [...] Report

# AboutDualStream

Built for streamers. By streamers.

## Our Story

DualStream is the streaming studio for going live everywhere — Twitch, YouTube, Kick, and any RTMP destination. Desktop and mobile, from one scene, in one click. Beautifully powerful. Astonishingly easy.

We were tired of choosing between desktop and mobile viewers. Tired of unstable streaming stacks held together by plugins. Tired of watching half our potential audience scroll past because we picked the wrong aspect ratio.

So in 2025, we built DualStream. Not because we're heroes (though we'll accept the title), but because streaming to everyone shouldn't require a computer science degree and 15 plugins. [...] ### Ryan Ippolito

Chief Financial Officer

30 years scaling high-growth companies. Helped raise $250M+ in capital and led $300M+ in M&A. Formerly CFO at Terumo Neuro (~$1B); leadership roles at Johnson & Johnson and Convatec.

> “I've been streaming since before vertical was even a thing, and every 'multi-format' tool I've tried bolted it on as an afterthought. DualStream is the first one that feels like it was built for the way I actually stream now.”

— @Tangent, Co-Founder & CGO · twitch.tv/tangent

## Our Mission

Make professional streaming tools accessible to everyone. No PhD required.

Simple

No complicated setup. Just stream.

Fast

New features every 2 weeks. Your input shapes what we build next.

Dual-Format

Stream desktop and mobile simultaneously.

## Get in Touch

results.title: DualStream — The easy streaming studio for every screen.
results.content:
## 2. How We Use Your Information

### A. To Provide and Operate the Service

 Account setup, authentication, security
 Enabling streaming to one or multiple destinations simultaneously (Twitch, YouTube, Kick, Custom RTMP, and others)
 Capturing camera and microphone audio/video (with your permission) in the mobile app and transmitting it in real time to your selected streaming destinations
 Managing linked third-party platform accounts and OAuth tokens
 Relaying chat messages from your connected platforms exclusively to your own authenticated DualStream application sessions in real-time
 Rendering dual-format output scenes (Desktop + Mobile)
 Tracking stream session analytics (duration, viewer counts, performance metrics)

### B. To Improve and Optimize the Service [...] ### A. Camera and Microphone

With your permission, the Mobile App captures video from your device cameras and audio from your microphone to show your live preview and to stream. When you stream using the DualStream relay, your audio/video is transmitted in real time to DualStream ingest servers, which forward it to the streaming platforms you have selected. When you stream to a custom RTMP destination, your audio/video is transmitted directly from your device to the endpoint you configure. DualStream does not record or persistently store your audio/video content; it is processed in real time for delivery only.

You can revoke camera and microphone access at any time in iOS Settings. Doing so disables streaming features but does not affect the rest of the app. [...] Account data: Retained until you delete your account or after 24 months of inactivity
 Crash logs and diagnostics: 12–24 months
 Stream session analytics: Up to 12 months, then anonymized or deleted
 Support communications: Up to 24 months
 OAuth tokens (all platforms): Deleted immediately when you disconnect a platform or delete your account
 Chat messages (all platforms): Relayed in real-time only; not persistently stored

For platform-specific retention details, see Section 4 above.

## 9. Your Rights

### Under GDPR / UK GDPR

Access, rectify, delete, restrict processing, object to processing, portability, withdraw consent, file a complaint with supervisory authorities.

### Under CCPA/CPRA

results.title: Dual Stream Camera: Why? - IPVM Discussions
results.content:
That being said, any of these could have been an edge recording stream, or mobile, but sometimes it is nice to keep them seperated so one change does not impact another...

Reactions:

Avatar

Marc Pichaud

•Dec 14, 2014

Some confusion might come from memorized profiles inside the camera (combination of pre settings) that you can call by cgi commands, Axis is doing since the begining, and real simultaneous multiple streams ... some cameras share their CPU to try to deliver the maximum, fps and res + motion + wdr + .., but sometimes you will never get what you expect, because ... it won't even leave the Ethernet interface ..... (some cameras tells you I send 70 Mbits, but when you measure it ... you get 45 Mbit max..) [...] Reactions:

UM

Undisclosed Manufacturer #3

•Dec 11, 2014

Another benefit of multi-streaming is that most PCs (usually onsite) can not handle either video card or CPU decoding many high megapixel cameras at once. The system chokes when you display 16+FullHD or 3Mp or 5Mp cameras at once. In addition, the monitor resolution does not allow you to see the full detail. Also, many camera at these high megapixels do not show 30 frames per second. Thus, monitor can look choppy and hard to watch.

By using a second stream, you lower the load the PC has in addition to the bandwidth, and can view a high frame rate, low bandwidth stream. [...] Since the live video is uploaded to the internet and to a remote site, smaller bandwidth means less expensive internet connections, etc.

The second stream (to the local storage recorder) is likely much more bandwidth, but higher FPS/lower compression/higher resolution. Since local storage means bandwidth in not restricted by an ISP (and transported a comparatively short distance), it makes sense to use more bandwidth.

Presumably, if some event is noticed remotely by the monitoring staff, they can download or review the 'higher quality' recording from onsite storage. They can do this only when needed, so 'dual streaming' helps make things more economical.

That's just one angle to this, and if there are more questions, just ask!
